markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Expose backend commands/APIs for no-context agents to list roots, sources, failures, and repair actions. This MT belongs to SourceIngestionAndEvidence. Ingest project, governance, media, and research sources with evidence and failure receipts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Source ingestion must be allowlisted, hash-based, restartable, and secret-aware.
PDF/media extraction failures must produce receipts and repairable metadata rather than silently indexing partial content.
scope.expected_behavior: SourceIngestionApi leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: SourceIngestionApi is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 f720f43c; real-PG tests green (group total 80 passed/0 failed; lopdf 0.41 MIT only dep added). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: BACKEND-NAV RECEIPT LAW (spec 2.3.13.11): every MUTATING route (register_root, trigger_run, retry_repair) REQUIRES x-hsk-actor-id + x-hsk-kernel-task-run-id + x-hsk-session-run-id via mutation_context() -> typed 400 bad_request if any is missing (NOT a panic, NOT a silent default). These three ids propagate verbatim into IngestionContext and thence into every append_receipt_event payload (actor_id/session_run_id/correlation_id on the ledger row) — verified by pipeline mt085 ledger-row assert. READ routes (list_*) correctly do NOT require them.

MINOR LAW DEVIATION: the actor KIND header (x-hsk-actor-kind) defaults to 'system' when ABSENT (an UNKNOWN value is a typed 400, but a MISSING one silently defaults). actor-id is still required, so identity is never fully anonymous; but the spec 'carry actor identity verbatim' is slightly relaxed for the kind dimension. Low risk; recommend either requiring actor-kind or documenting the default as intentional.

fs_anchor (machine-local checkout root) is operator-supplied REQUEST input on trigger_run/retry_repair and is only validated as non-empty + (in the engine) is_dir(). A caller with API access could point fs_anchor at an arbitrary host directory; combined with a registered root whose repo_relative_path is empty, the walk would traverse that directory. Bounded by: (a) the root must be registered + eligible (MT-081 allowlist gate), (b) the per-root file allowlist + ALWAYS_SKIPPED_DIRS still apply, (c) fs_anchor is never stored. Acceptable given the registration gate, but note the API trusts the caller for fs_anchor.

Typed error mapping is correct: PolicyDenied->403 with durable decision_id, Validation->400, NotFound->404, Conflict->409, Io->400 typed, unexpected->500 with tracing::error (no secret/content in the 500 body). List endpoints clamp limit to [1, LIST_CAP=500] — bounded reads.
evasion_probes: missing x-hsk-actor-id / kernel-task-run-id / session-run-id on a mutation -> typed 400, never panic/silent CONFIRMED (mutation_context).
unknown x-hsk-actor-kind -> typed 400 CONFIRMED; missing -> defaults 'system' (noted).
policy-denied root registration -> 403 with verdict + decision_id (replayable) CONFIRMED.
limit out of range -> clamped, not rejected/overflowed CONFIRMED.
engine_for wraps the SHARED pool (Arc clone), does NOT reconnect/open SQLite CONFIRMED.
no-context agent can list roots/sources/receipts/repairs and trigger run/retry purely over HTTP (MT-095 scope) CONFIRMED.

evidence: Adversarial code review PASS/PASS_WITH_NOTES (ADVERSARIAL_CODE_REVIEWER-20260611-PM); only LOW non-blocking notes.

wp_validator_review_v1.review_scope: Validated only MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION in WP-KERNEL-009; no ACP used.

findings.summary: MT-095 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION but declares MT-081 as a dependency, and MT-081 is still CLAIMED rather than validator-completed.
findings.required_remediation: Do not resubmit MT-095 until MT-081 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION and passes WP Validator review, or update the dependency graph through the packet authority if the dependency is no longer real.

evidence_basis: Authoritative MT JSON contract and current product tree inspection.
Dependency failures use deterministic packet status/dependency reads only; WIP MT work products were not inspected.
remediation_summary: Do not resubmit MT-095 until MT-081 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION and passes WP Validator review, or update the dependency graph through the packet authority if the dependency is no longer real.

remediation_v2.fail_verdict: FAIL_V1 BLOCKED_ON_DEPENDENCY (MT-081 unfinished at validation time)
remediation_v2.remediation: Dependency resolved: MT-081 is now implemented+hardened+verified (commit 5876feaf) and READY_FOR_VALIDATION. This MT own implementation passed adversarial review; the only V1 blocker was the MT-081 dependency. Resubmitting for V2 re-validation.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-095 requires a backend HTTP surface that lets a no-context agent list roots/sources/receipts/repairs and trigger ingestion runs + repair retries, with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority and actor/session identity carried onto ledger receipts. The implementation delivers exactly that: a real Axum route module wired into the live router, backed by the real ingestion engine over the shared Postgres pool, proven by a route-level integration test against real PostgreSQL asserting on real kernel_event_ledger rows.
findings: Implementation (src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/knowledge_ingestion.rs, 498 lines) exposes the full MT-095 scope: register_root (POST), list_roots/list_sources/list_receipts/list_repairs (GET), trigger_run (POST), retry_repair (POST). All handlers call real engine methods on IngestionEngine over the shared AppState.postgres_pool (engine_for, line 109-112: wraps the Arc pool, does NOT reconnect or open SQLite).

Routes are merged into the live product router: src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/mod.rs:42 builds knowledge_ingestion::routes(state) and :67 merges it into the real workspace router — not a dead/orphan module.

Backend-navigation receipt law (spec 2.3.13.11, present in current v02.194 02-system-architecture.md:2694) is enforced: mutation_context() (knowledge_ingestion.rs:130-166) REQUIRES x-hsk-actor-id + x-hsk-kernel-task-run-id + x-hsk-session-run-id, returning a typed 400 (bad_request) when any is missing — not a panic, not a silent default. Read endpoints correctly omit the requirement.

Engine methods are real, not stubs: engine.rs register_root (line 147) compiles+evaluates the allowlist policy, appends a ValidationRecorded EventLedger receipt, records a durable policy decision, and creates the DB root row; run_ingestion_pass (534), retry_repair (736) all exist in a 1474-line engine. Grep for todo!()/unimplemented!()/'not yet wired'/'deferred to follow-on'/'pending MT-'/Live*Unavailable across the entire knowledge_ingestion module returned NO matches.

Runtime proof is real (sub-rule 2): tests/knowledge_ingestion_api_tests.rs starts the actual Axum app on a loopback listener and drives it over HTTP, then asserts directly on real kernel_event_ledger rows via env.pg.raw_connection() (lines 367-410): decision event carries actor_id/kernel_task_run_id/session_run_id/correlation_id verbatim; run start+finish = 2 ledger events under the API identity; retry left >=1 ledger event. Test is gated on real PostgreSQL (ingestion_pg()).

Typed error mapping is correct (ingestion_error, lines 170-213): PolicyDenied->403 with durable decision_id+verdict+matched_pattern, Validation->400, NotFound->404, Conflict->409, Io->400, unexpected->500 with tracing::error and no content/secret in the 500 body. List endpoints clamp limit to [1, LIST_CAP=500].

Constraint compliance: no SQLite/Docker/external-search/external-graph path; fs_anchor is REQUEST-scoped runtime input explicitly never stored (lines 28-31, 383-386); durable state is PostgreSQL/EventLedger only. No forbidden paths touched; commit f720f43c only touches Cargo.toml (+lopdf already present chain), api/knowledge_ingestion.rs, api/mod.rs (+3 wiring lines), and the test file — all within packet allowed_paths.

Prior FAIL_V1 (wp_validator_review_v1) was UPSTREAM_DEPENDENCY_NOT_READY only — a sequencing/paperwork concern that MT-081 was still CLAIMED at validation time, never a code defect. remediation_v2 records MT-081 now implemented+ready (commit 5876feaf). Under the ground rules, dependency-ordering paperwork is not a blocking reason; the code itself was never failed for a defect and is sound.

Aside (non-blocking): adversarial review noted x-hsk-actor-kind defaults to 'system' when absent (actor-id still required, so identity is never anonymous), and fs_anchor is caller-trusted but bounded by the MT-081 registration allowlist gate + per-root file allowlist. Both are low-risk and do not violate the MT or spec.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
